compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
# Compaction: rename folded events to `*.retired` (invariant I1)

## Why
Compaction hard-deletes the event files it folds (`compact.py:140-144` `os.remove`). A delete/add
reconciliation (a rebase ‚Äî the RC1 class) can resurrect a folded source event -> `SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT`.
`docs/concurrency.md` I1 already SAYS compaction must rename folded files to `*.retired`; the code does not.
Prerequisite for A2 (glandulous-potent-waxwing) rebuild backstop and A3 (entitled-unsweet-nutria) remediation.

## What
- Add ONE shared contract: `RETIRED_SUFFIX = ".retired"` + `is_active_event(name)` helper (in
  `reducer/_cache.py` or a small util), imported by compaction, the reducer, and fsck ‚Äî single source of truth.
- `compact.py:140-144` ‚Äî replace `os.remove(fp)` with `os.rename(fp, fp + RETIRED_SUFFIX)`.
- ORDERING/IDEMPOTENCY: write the SNAPSHOT atomically FIRST, then rename; a re-run after a mid-loop crash
  skips files already `*.retired` (the SNAPSHOT-present short-circuit at compact.py:227-231 / _scan_snapshot_state
  :266-268 makes a re-compact a no-op). Race guard: `if os.path.exists(fp + RETIRED_SUFFIX): continue`.
- ERROR HANDLING: do NOT silently swallow `OSError` ‚Äî log WARNING per failure / INFO per success so a partial
  rename is observable; on failure, reverse already-completed renames before re-raising (atomic).
- `reducer/_cache.py` ‚Äî exclude `*.retired` in BOTH listing paths: the `event_filenames` list (~line 97) AND
  the `*.json` glob (~line 102), via `is_active_event`.
- `fsck.py` ‚Äî explicit `is_active_event` guard in the `_check_snapshot` loop; `SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT` must NOT
  fire on a retired source file.
- `docs/concurrency.md` (I1) ‚Äî align doc with code; ADD a `.retired` lifecycle note: retention permanent for
  now (accepted storage tradeoff); branch-wide `.retired` GC is a documented FOLLOW-UP (new linked ticket),
  safe only past causal stability. State that `.retired` files are benign under a code rollback (old `*.json`
  glob ignores them).

## Scope
Compaction + reducer/fsck `.retired` exclusion + the shared constant/helper + the I1 doc. Does NOT change
replay ordering (A2) or remediate the live store (A3).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Step 0 (RED): write the two-clone merge+rebase resurrection test; record it FAILING on unpatched code.
- [ ] `RETIRED_SUFFIX` + `is_active_event` defined once and used by compaction, reducer (both listing paths), fsck. Proof: `grep -rn "\.retired" src/rebar | grep -v RETIRED_SUFFIX` shows only the constant def.
- [ ] `os.remove` gone from the fold loop. Proof: `grep -n "os.remove" src/rebar/_commands/compact.py` ‚Äî no fold-loop match.
- [ ] SNAPSHOT written before renames; simulated mid-loop-crash re-run is a no-op (idempotent); rename failures logged (not swallowed) and reversed (atomic).
- [ ] Reducer replay + `fsck` on a store with a `*.retired` file exit clean (no `SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT`); fresh replay equals the pre-compaction snapshot.
- [ ] Two-clone merge+rebase resurrection regression test now GREEN.
- [ ] `docs/concurrency.md` I1 matches code; `.retired` GC follow-up ticket created + linked.
compiled_state.title: Compaction: non-destructive `*.retired` rename (invariant I1)

verify_commands.dd_text: folded files renamed to .retired (not deleted); two-clone resurrection test passes
